rulings.Case Title: CAL-MURPHY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. HINES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer Of Cross-Defts George And Jeanette Omran To Nop 560 Mission Llc'S Cross-Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 11, 2009, line 11, CROSS DEFENDANTS GEORGE and JEANETTE OMRAN'S Demurrer. Demurrer is off calendar as moot in view of court's ruling on motion pursuant to CCP section 425.16. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CAL-MURPHY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. HINES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Cross-Deft Murphy'S Deli Franchsing, Inc.'S Motion Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16 To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 11, 2009, line 11, CROSS-DEFENDANT MURPHY'S DELI FRANCHISING INC.'S Motion Pursuant To CCP Section 425.16 To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complaint. Is granted. Cross-complaint arises out of protected activity and is designed to inhibit litigation. An act in furtherance of a first amendment right comprises both a right to speak freely and also a right to refrain from doing so at all. ARP Pharmacy Services, Inc. vs. Gallagher Bassett Services, Inc. (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 1307, 1314. Here the gravamen of NOP's cross-complaint is defendant's failure to prevent the filing of the underlying complaint. Cross complainant fails to demonstrate the probability of prevailing on its claims. Cross-complainant must demonstrate that the cross-complaint is both legally sufficient and supported by a prima facie showing of facts to sustain a favorable judgment. Feldman vs. 1100 Park Lane Associates (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1467. There is no allegation of a particular breach of the lease by Murphy's that would give rise to a breach of contract cause of action or facts pled to trigger an indemnity provision. No facts alleged describing a controversy that would support a declaratory relief cause of action. The cross-complaint is barred by the litigation privilege in that it is based on allegedly wrongful conduct by cross-defendants' failure to prevent a lawsuit and its purpose was designed to chill free access to the courts. Cross-complaint's Notice of Errata not considered as untimely. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CAL-MURPHY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. HINES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16 To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 11, 2009, line 11, CROSS-DEFENANTS GEORGE AND JEANETTE OMRAN'S Motion Pursuant To CCP Section 425.16 To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complaint. Is Granted. Cross-complaint arises out of protected activity and is designed to inhibit litigation. An act in furtherance of a first amendment right comprises both a right to speak freely and also a right to refrain from doing so at all. ARP Pharmacy Services, Inc. vs. Gallagher Bassett Services, Inc. (2006) 138 Cal.App. 4th 1307, 1314. Here the gravamen of NOP's cross-complaint is defendants' failure to prevent the filing of the underlying complaint. Cross-complainant fails to demonstrate the probability of prevailing on its claims. Cross-complainant must demonstrate that the cross-complaint is both legally sufficient and supported by a prima facie showing of facts to sustain a favorable judgment. Feldman vs. 1100 Park Lane Associates (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1467. There is no allegation of a particular breach of the lease by Cal-Murphy that would trigger the Guaranty Agreement and establish a breach. No facts alleged giving rise to a controversy that would support a declaratory relief cause of action. The cross-complaint is barred by the litigation privilege in that it is based on allegedly wrongful conduct by cross-defendants' failure to prevent a lawsuit, and its purpose was designed to chill free access to the courts. Cross-complaint's Notice of Errata not considered as untimely. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CHIATELLO VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 11, 2009, line 10, Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Demurrer to Complaint. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff lacks standing to sue. Court may not grant injunctive relief to prevent tax collection. (See Darr vs. Alvord (1980) 101 Cal.App. 3d 480). Plaintiff fails to plead he is injured or will be injured by Proposition Q. Any party who may be injured by Proposition Q has an adequate remedy to challenge its validity by paying the tax first and then bringing a claim for refund before seeking judicial relief. Leave to amend is denied. The text of the amendments to section 902.1(d) was included in the Voter pamphlet, and the effect of Proposition Q was adequately explained to voters. No reasonable likelihood that plaintiff will be able to state a valid cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA IRONWORKERS FIELD PENSION TRUST et al VS. 195 WILLOW STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 11, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT 195 WILLOW STREET INVESTMENTS, LLC, 818 VAN NESS, LLC, and, 81-816-826 VAN NESS LP DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs were not required to file a preliminary twenty day notice under CC section 3097(b). Plaintiffs are not entitled to the extra protections afforded to original contractors and claimants described in CC section 3259.5(a). Plaintiffs are subject to the thirty day deadline set forth in CC section 3116 with which they failed to comply. No reasonable probability that the defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)
